items.title: LeBron James to Miami Heat? YouTube page leaks future press conference
items.summary: LeBron James is going back to the Miami Heat ― at least that's what many are speculating after the organization's latest slip up. The Miami Heat's official YouTube page briefly leaked a live video stream titled "LeBron James Introductory Press Conference" that was set for July 27, 2026.It has since been unlisted and made private. It seems that the video has been momentarily taken down, but not fast enough for internet scourers to collect their screenshots, screen-records and begin running with the narrative that James is headed back to the 305. .oembed-frame {width:100%;height:100%;margin:0;border:0;}The Miami Heat YouTube channel just accidentally LEAKED the LeBron signing 😱😳🚨 pic.twitter.com/qqKjC5b70m— Basketball Forever (@bballforever_) July 22, 2026If it's true, then at least we know that there will be an announcement within the next six days. However, it seems that it was a…

items.title: Naismith Basketball Hall of Fame announces new Stephen Curry exhibit
items.summary: It's a no-brainer that whenever Golden State Warriors guard Stephen Curry decides to hang it up for good, he'll be inducted into the Naismith Basketball Hall of Fame on the first ballot.In case his career statistics and accolades didn't signal that, the Basketball Hall of Fame practically ensured it when they announced that the four-time NBA champion will have his own exhibit, marking the first time an active player was honored with a dedicated experience.The Naismith Basketball Hall of Fame will introduce its newest exhibit on Friday, July 24, which will be dedicated to the 12-time NBA All-Star, two-time MVP, Olympic gold medalist, four-time NBA champion and the undisputed greatest shooter of all time.This development is a part of the Naismith Basketball Hall of Fame acclaimed gallery called "The Vault."The exhibit is called "Stephen Curry: Beyond the…

items.title: Warriors' Yaxel Lendeborg earns Summer League MVP, first team honors
items.summary: Golden State Warriors rookie Yaxel Lendeborg has had himself quite the summer, going back to training camp with a Summer League championship and accolades to accompany it. Lendeborg was named the best player throughout the NBA summer showcase, selected as the 2026 Summer League MVP.He was also named the Summer League championship game MVP after posting a 21-point, 10-rebound performance in a 94-90 victory against the Memphis Grizzlies on July 19. Lendeborg is the second Warrior to win the Summer League championship game MVP, joining Ian Clark, who did it 2013. However, he is the first player in Golden State Warriors history to win the Summer League MVP. The award was first introduced in 2006."It means a lot. You know, it comes from all the sacrifices, all the struggle to get to this point," Lendeborg said after the Summer League championship on July 19. "Every game I play, I have…

items.title: Stephen Curry to become first active player with exhibit in Basketball Hall of Fame
items.summary: Stephen Curry is in the Naismith Basketball Hall of Fame.His official induction is unquestionably coming but more than half a decade away, four years after he retires. However, he is about to become the first active player with an exhibit in the Hall, the institution announced."Stephen Curry: Beyond the Arc" will open July 24, and is a celebration of Curry, the four-time NBA champion, two-time MVP, 12-time NBA All-Star, Olympic gold medalist and the NBA's career leader in 3-pointers made."Being part of the Basketball Hall of Fame in this way is hard to put into words," Curry said in a statement announcing the exhibit. "When I think about my journey, I think about the people who believed in me, the work that happened when nobody was watching and the moments that shaped me on and off the court. To have that story told while I'm still playing is something I…

items.title: How Victor Wembanyama reacted when asked to sign Jalen Brunson jersey in awkward Fanatics Fest moment
items.summary: wembyAn awkward moment was captured on video between Spurs star Victor Wembanyama and a young Knicks fan at Fanatics Fest.Wembanyama, 22, walked past a group of fans during the weekend event, and one young person called out, “Wemby! Wemby! Sign my jersey! Sign my jersey please!…Wemby…will you sign my jersey?”It sounds like a normal request, but there was a catch.The fan was holding out a Jalen Brunson Knicks jersey.Kid: “Wemby! Wemby! Sign my jersey! Sign my jersey please!…Wemby…will you sign my jersey?”Wemby: “That’s not…That’s…Jalen Brunson…”Kid: “Sorry”(: milaxnyc) pic.twitter.com/tb3wYA4jCB— New York Basketball (@NBA_NewYork) July 21, 2026Victor Wembanyama attends the Men in Blazers panel at Fanatics Fest NYC 2026 at Jacob Javits Center on July 18, 2026 in New York City.
